description: PGN Support - Parse Portable Game Notation in chesslib and expose pgn-fen / pgn-match subcommands in chess-runner to load games from any position.

userStories.title: PGN parser in chesslib
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a chesslib function that parses a PGN string into a sequence of ChessMove values so that any crate can replay games without duplicating parsing logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Module `chesslib::pgn` exists and is declared in `chesslib/src/lib.rs`
`chesslib::pgn::parse(pgn: &str) -> Result<Vec<ChessMove>, PgnError>` is public and parses a single-game PGN string
Parses standard SAN move tokens: pawn pushes (e4), pawn captures (exd5), piece moves (Nf3), captures (Qxh7), castling (O-O, O-O-O), promotions (e8=Q), and decorators (+, #) stripped silently
Strips PGN tag headers ([Tag "Value"] lines) and move numbers (e.g. `1.`) before parsing tokens
Strips inline comments ({ ... }) and annotation glyphs (!, ?, !!, ??, !?, ?!) silently
`PgnError` implements `std::error::Error` and is convertible to `anyhow::Error` via `?`
Returns `PgnError` with a descriptive message on invalid or ambiguous SAN
Convenience wrapper `parse()` calls `parse_nth(pgn, 0)` internally
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Multi-game PGN selection with parse_nth
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to select a specific game by index from a multi-game PGN file so that I can load any game in a collection.
acceptanceCriteria: `chesslib::pgn::parse_nth(pgn: &str, game_index: usize) -> Result<Vec<ChessMove>, PgnError>` is public
Selects the game at 0-based `game_index`; returns `PgnError` if the index is out of range
Games are delimited by PGN tag headers — each new `[Event ...]` header (or any tag header following a result token) starts a new game
Unit test: a two-game PGN string returns different move lists for index 0 and index 1
Unit test: index out of range returns `Err(PgnError)`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Move replay to a target position
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to replay N half-moves from a parsed PGN so that I can obtain the Board at any point in the game.
acceptanceCriteria: `chesslib::pgn::replay(moves: &[ChessMove], up_to: usize) -> Result<Board, PgnError>` is public
`up_to = 0` returns the starting position (Board::default())
`up_to = moves.len()` returns the final position
Returns `PgnError` if `up_to > moves.len()`
Returns `PgnError` if any move in the prefix is illegal on the board at that point
Unit test: replaying 0 moves from any game returns the starting FEN
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Correctness test suite for PGN parsing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want automated tests that verify PGN parsing against known-correct positions so I can trust the parser.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one full real-world PGN game (e.g. a famous historical game) is embedded in the test module as a string literal
A test replays all moves and compares the final FEN against a pre-computed reference string
A test runs perft(1) on the final position and asserts the expected node count
A test exercises castling SAN: both O-O and O-O-O appear and parse correctly
A test exercises at least one promotion (e.g. e8=Q or equivalent)
A test exercises file-disambiguation (e.g. Rdf8) and rank-disambiguation (e.g. R1a3) if present in the chosen game, or via a synthetic FEN position
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: chess-runner pgn-fen subcommand
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run `chess-runner pgn-fen --file game.pgn --move 20` and get the FEN string printed to stdout so I can pipe it into other tools.
acceptanceCriteria: `pgn-fen` is a first-class clap subcommand in chess-runner alongside match, sprt, report, version-report
Accepts `--file <path>` (required), `--move <N>` (optional, defaults to final position), `--game <N>` (optional, 1-based index, default 1)
`--move 0` prints the starting FEN (rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/8/8/PPPPPPPP/RNBQKBNR w KQkq - 0 1)
Prints only the FEN string to stdout (no trailing label or extra text) so it can be piped directly
Exits with non-zero code and a human-readable error on: file not found, parse failure, `--move` out of range, `--game` out of range
The `--game` flag is 1-based in the CLI; the implementation passes `game - 1` to `parse_nth`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: chess-runner pgn-match subcommand
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run a two-engine match starting from a position loaded from a PGN file so I can test engine behavior on specific mid-game or endgame scenarios.
acceptanceCriteria: `pgn-match` is a first-class clap subcommand in chess-runner

Accepts `--file <path>` (required), `--move <N>` (optional, defaults to final position), `--game <N>` (optional, 1-based, default 1), plus `--engine1`, `--engine2`, `--games`, `--time`, `--inc`, `--timeout`, `--csv` (same as `match` subcommand)
Reconstructs the FEN via `pgn::parse_nth` + `pgn::replay` and passes it as `start_fen` to the Match runner
When `--games > 1`, engines alternate colors each game (same behavior as `match`)
Match result is printed in the same format as `match` and optionally appended to CSV
Exits with non-zero code and a human-readable error on PGN load failure
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
